title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce may become a free agent after Indianapolis chose not to franchise tag him.
Pierce led the NFL in yards per catch for the last two seasons and had 47 catches for 1,003 yards in 2025.
The Patriots have $39.2 million in cap space and could pursue Pierce as a top receiver option.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, a Vermont postal worker, was cited for allegedly discarding mail intended for delivery.
Police found opened and unopened mail, including packages and holiday cards, in a North Troy dumpster.
Morisseau faces nine petty larceny and five unlawful mischief counts; she is suspended and due in court March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An elderly man and his dog died in a Foxborough house fire on Granite Street early Tuesday morning.
The fire, extinguished within an hour, caused major damage to the single-family home.
The cause of the fire remains under investigation by local fire officials and law enforcement.

title: Patriots players brought strippers and hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant. The owner is now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's liquor license is in jeopardy after hosting a Patriots party with strippers, hookahs, and after-hours drinking.
Police found about 30 people, strong tobacco and marijuana odors, and several nude women during a noise complaint investigation.
Owner Helder Brandao claims patriots players brought their own alcohol and lost cash; the Licensing Board will vote on fines.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved top nationwide AP scores, with 35.8% scoring 3 or higher, breaking an all-time record.
The state leads in AP exam accessibility, subsidizing over 90,783 exams for nearly 50,000 low-income students.
Massachusetts ranked third nationally in the percentage of graduates taking AP exams, emphasizing college credit opportunities.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy's new city councilors are collecting a $15,000 raise they initially promised to repeal after a historic election.
Councilors cannot immediately repeal the raises due to state laws and city charter constraints, but plan to donate the raise.
Several councilors, including At-Large Councilor Yuan, intend to donate their portion of the raise to nonprofits and charities.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox remain a potential trade destination for Astros' infielder Isaac Paredes, involving outfielder Jarren Duran.
Adding Paredes could boost the Red Sox's power, offering an alternative for third base alongside Caleb Durbin.
Despite interest, no recent trade discussions have occurred between the Red Sox and Astros regarding Isaac Paredes.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's Jordan Reid predicts Patriots will pick Cashius Howell, a Texas A&M edge rusher, in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season, ranking seventh among FBS defensive linemen, with 41 total pressures in 2025.
Patriots seeking a pass-rushing presence due to potential loss of K’Lavon Chaisson and an underperforming defensive line.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, found guilty of first-degree murder for the 1986 killing of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Carey was indicted in 2022 following new DNA evidence linking him to the crime scene from Gravel's clothing.
Carey, previously serving for attempted murder, faces life in prison without parole; sentencing set for March 26.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, is accused of shooting at a Black man who mistakenly drove to her Weare, N.H. property.
Durgin faces charges including criminal threatening and attempted first degree assault with a deadly weapon.
Prosecutors seek a preliminary injunction to prevent Durgin from contacting the victim and repeating the alleged behavior.
